theme: Case study: How a pharma company turned 80 employees into brand ambassadors

linkedin.text:
The brief: 80 field reps. Three months of record performance. A CEO who wanted something more than plaques and applause.

The challenge: These people had seen every classic incentive. Beach hotel. Awards dinner. Open bar. They were hard to impress.

What we built:

Budapest, 4 nights.

Night 1 — Thermal spa evening at Széchenyi baths. Champagne. Live music. Nobody on their phone.

Night 2 — Private dinner at the Hungarian Parliament. Forty-five minutes of the river view they'll describe for the next decade.

Day 3 — Team challenge: city scavenger hunt with a secret mission. Mixed teams. Friendly competition. Inside jokes born that morning still circulate in their WhatsApp groups.

Night 3 — Awards ceremony on a Danube river cruise. Each recipient heard a personalized 60-second tribute written by their direct manager.

What happened after:
- Sales team NPS (net promoter score) rose 18 points
- Zero resignations among participants for 14 months
- Three people told HR they turned down offers from competitors because of the trip

The CEO's exact words at debrief:
"We spent ₪220,000. We retained talent worth 10x that."

That's not event production. That's strategic investment.

📩 alon@upe.co.il

Alon Ouaknine

#CaseStudy #CorporateEvents #Budapest #IncentiveTravel #EmployeeRetention #HRStrategy #UproductionEvents #MICE
